pattern: A breadth-first sweep groups nodes by depth, so summing each level's values and dividing by its count yields the per-level averages in top-down order.
hints: All the nodes you need to combine for one answer entry sit on the same depth, so process the tree one full level at a time.
Keep a frontier of the current level's nodes; sum their values, divide by how many there are, then build the next frontier from their children.
Use a queue and, at each step, snapshot how many nodes are currently in it so you know exactly where the level boundary is before enqueuing children.

edge_inputs.description: negative and large values force averaging to a fraction

stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(1, max(1, size))
    vals = [rng.randint(-1000000, 1000000) for _ in range(n)]
    # build a near-complete tree array form: index i children 2i+1, 2i+2
    return [vals]
stress.note: a dense array form is a valid level-order tree with no missing internal slots
